title: Block data carries no schema_version — read-time validation and migration are inert for all blocks
description: Block data files carry no schema_version: the write path (writeTypedFile / writeBlockForDir) stamps only author/identity fields and never adds one, and no block schema defines a top-level schema_version (envelopes are additionalProperties:false, which rejects it). Version lives only in the schema file. With schema_version absent from every block's data, two safety mechanisms are inert for all blocks: the read-time AJV hook (gated on schema_version presence, block-api.ts:785-789) never fires, and the version-mismatch branch of validateBlockWithMigrationForDir (schema-validator.ts:225-239) never triggers. Block schema-evolution safety runs only via the explicit install/update path (baseline content_hash-tracked), never on read. The class: read-time validation and migration are inert whenever the versioned-data envelope is absent.

evidence.reference: writeTypedFile / writeBlockForDir stamp author/identity fields only; schema_version is carried through only if the incoming data already has it — nothing in the write path produces one
representative block schema: declares only the array key as a top-level property under additionalProperties:false; the version lives in the schema file's version field, not in the data envelope
the read-time AJV hook is gated on schema_version being present in the block data; with it absent the hook never fires
validateBlockWithMigrationForDir's version-mismatch branch compares schema.version to data.schema_version; with data.schema_version absent it never triggers

impact: Block data is validated at write (whole-block AJV) and by explicit context-validate, but never on read and never migrated on a version gap, because schema_version is absent. A schema-evolution change is invisible until the next write to that block, and a catalog-declared version-bump migration never applies to existing block data on read. Block schema evolution has no read-time safety.
canonical_vocabulary: versioned data envelope / schema_version stamping (read-time version-gated validation + forward migration)
proposed_resolution: Stamp the current schema version onto the block envelope (top-level schema_version) on the write path, and admit a single optional top-level schema_version property on each block schema envelope (a named relaxation of the envelope additionalProperties:false, not a wholesale opening; per-item objects are unchanged). The existing read-time AJV hook (block-api.ts:785-789) and the migration version-mismatch branch (schema-validator.ts:225-239) then become active for all blocks: on a version gap, a registered migration walks the whole block forward, and absent one the read/write gate throws. Per-item stamping is out of scope (per the block-api.ts:782-784 comment) — it is a distinct, larger task.
